title: Steamed chocolate pudding
subtitle: A rich, dense steamed sponge with chocolate sauce
excerpt: A steamed sponge pudding made with cocoa, deeply moist and dense in the way only a pudding basin and a long steam can produce. Turned out hot with chocolate sauce poured over, the sponge cuts cleanly and holds its shape on the plate. It takes about 2 hours to steam, most of which is hands-off.
sourceNotes: Steamed puddings are a British tradition documented from at least the seventeenth century. Isabella Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg, gives numerous steamed sponge and suet pudding recipes. The chocolate version follows the standard steamed sponge formula adapted for cocoa. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), also documents the steamed basin method.

recipe.freezeNotes: Steam and cool completely, then wrap tightly and freeze. Steam from frozen for 45 minutes to reheat.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Steam, cool, and refrigerate up to 2 days ahead. Reheat by steaming for 30 minutes.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: steamed on the hob, not oven-baked

content.text: A steamed chocolate pudding is a dense, moist sponge cooked in a covered basin over simmering water. The steam heats it gently and evenly, giving a texture that no oven can replicate: deeply tender, with no crust and no dry edges. Turned out hot and served with chocolate sauce or custard, it is the standard of cold-weather British puddings.
Butter a 1.2-litre pudding basin thoroughly and dust with cocoa powder. Cut a circle of baking paper to fit the base. Have a sheet of foil ready for the cover.

Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well between each. Sift in the flour and cocoa, then fold in gently. Add the milk and vanilla and fold until smooth. Pour into the prepared basin; the batter should come three-quarters of the way up.

Cover the basin tightly with a double layer of foil, making a pleat in the centre to allow for expansion. Secure with string under the rim. Lower the basin into a large saucepan with a trivet or upturned saucer underneath. Pour in boiling water to come halfway up the sides. Cover the pan and steam on a steady simmer for 2 hours, topping up the water every 45 minutes.
Heat the cream until just steaming. Pour over the chopped chocolate, leave for 1 minute, then stir from the centre until smooth. Add the butter and stir until glossy.

Remove the basin from the pan and rest for 5 minutes. Run a palette knife around the edge and invert onto a deep serving plate. The pudding should drop cleanly; if it sticks, give the base a sharp knock. Pour the chocolate sauce over immediately. Serve with custard or cream alongside.
